prompt: PowerSync Discord ticket: a Tesla Powerwall user disabled Smart Optimization while their EV was charging during a scheduled EV preserve window. The Powerwall backup reserve stayed at 74% (matching SOC at that moment) instead of returning to the configured 20%. Trace the code path that raises the reserve and the path that fails to restore it on disable.
assertions: Identifies that Tesla lacks set_no_discharge_mode so EV preserve falls back to _set_idle_hold_mode(preserve_charge=True), which raises the hardware backup reserve to about the current SOC and snapshots the user's reserve in _pre_idle_backup_reserve
Identifies that disable() gates its idle cleanup on _last_executed_action == 'idle' while the marker is 'no_discharge', so _restore_pre_idle_backup_reserve is never called
Identifies that the EV release path (_release_scheduled_ev_no_discharge_mode / executor stop) only restores the work mode and never lowers the Tesla backup reserve
Notes there is no later rescue: the startup/reload self-heal excludes Tesla and the optimizer is now disabled so no cycle repairs it
Fix outline restores the pre-idle backup reserve in disable() whenever _pre_idle_backup_reserve is set (ungated from the 'idle' marker)
